skill.name: code-review-preferences
skill.description: Guides code reviews with a three-pass method, team standards, intake questions, and concise feedback rules.
skill.summary: Apply structured code review preferences for PRs, code quality discussions, and team feedback.
skill.author: ChaiWithJai

security_audit.summary: The six static findings are false positives from normal review guidance text in SKILL.md, including words such as description, descriptive, and Describe. No executable code, commands, network access, prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration pattern, or cryptographic implementation is present.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: False positive. The flagged lines contain prose in a review checklist, not cryptographic code or weak algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is natural language about review standards and intake questions. I found no imports, functions, keys, hashes, ciphers, or encryption operations.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: False positive. The flagged lines describe review phases and test checks, not network scanning or host discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains only Markdown guidance and no network commands, endpoints, sockets, scanners, or reconnaissance workflow.

content.value_statement: Code reviews can become inconsistent or overly subjective. This skill applies a concise review method and clear feedback standards for PRs and code discussions.
actual_capabilities: Uses a three-pass review method: understand, correctness, then improvements.
Prioritizes bugs, tests, edge cases, security issues, and secrets in code.
Limits improvement feedback to a maximum of five comments per review.
Prompts for review format, change context, and specific concerns before starting.
Guides reviewers toward specific questions and away from vague or personal style comments.
limitations: Does not run tests, inspect builds, or verify runtime behavior.
Requires the user to provide code, a diff, or a clear PR description.
Does not replace a dedicated security review for sensitive systems.
Uses general review standards unless project-specific rules are provided.

use_cases.title: Review a pull request
use_cases.description: Apply a structured checklist to find bugs, missing tests, edge cases, and security concerns before merge.

use_cases.title: Standardize team feedback
use_cases.description: Use shared review language so comments stay specific, respectful, and focused on maintainability.

use_cases.title: Coach junior reviewers
use_cases.description: Teach reviewers how to understand scope first, then assess correctness before suggesting improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PR using the code-review-preferences skill. Focus on correctness, tests, edge cases, and clear feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this bug fix. Confirm the root cause is addressed, edge cases are covered, and no regression risk is obvious.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a change with sensitive data or access control
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change with special attention to security issues, secrets in code, input handling, and failure modes.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a larger feature or refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature as a senior reviewer. First summarize scope, then assess correctness, maintainability, tests, and the top five improvement comments.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds a new payment validation flow.
output: Scope: New payment validation behavior and related error handling.
Correctness: Check null inputs, duplicate submissions, and declined payment responses.
Suggested comment: What happens if the payment provider times out after the order is created?

output_examples.input: A refactor splits a large service into smaller modules.
output: Scope: Internal structure changed without intended behavior changes.
Correctness: Compare old and new error paths, dependency injection, and transaction boundaries.
Suggested comment: Can we add a regression test for the previous service behavior before merging?

output_examples.input: A junior reviewer asks how to comment on unclear naming.
output: Use a question instead of a command.
Suggested comment: Would a name that includes the domain concept make this condition easier to scan?
Avoid style-only feedback unless it affects readability or consistency.

best_practices: Provide the diff, PR goal, and risk areas before asking for review.
Ask for correctness feedback before requesting style or cleanup suggestions.
Share project-specific standards when they differ from the skill defaults.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to enforce personal formatting preferences without team agreement.
Asking for a final approval decision without providing enough context.
Replacing automated tests or security checks with conversational review only.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and feedback structure only.

faq.question: Can it review pull requests in Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide PR review discussions in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it find every security issue?
faq.answer: No. It prompts for security review, but it is not a full security scanner.

faq.question: How many comments should a review include?
faq.answer: The skill recommends no more than five improvement comments per review.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the code or diff, the change type, and any specific concerns.

faq.question: Can teams customize the standards?
faq.answer: Yes. Add project rules or review policies when asking for a review.
